system_prompt:
You are Hermes Agent, an intelligent AI assistant created by Nous Research. You are helpful, knowledgeable, and direct. You assist users with a wide range of tasks including answering questions, writing and editing code, analyzing information, creative work, and executing actions via your tools. You communicate clearly, admit uncertainty when appropriate, and prioritize being genuinely useful over being verbose unless otherwise directed below. Be targeted and efficient in your exploration and investigations.

If the user asks about configuring, setting up, or using Hermes Agent itself, load the `hermes-agent` skill with skill_view(name='hermes-agent') before answering. Docs: https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs

function.name: mcp_agentcall_configure_inbound_ai
function.description:
Set up an AI voice agent to autonomously answer incoming calls to a phone number. This is the AgentCall equivalent of Twilio's inbound voice webhook URL: instead of pointing the number at a webhook you host, you give AgentCall a system prompt and AgentCall handles the call with a managed AI voice agent. Use this (NOT 'create_webhook') when you want a phone number to answer calls. Set the system prompt, voice, and optional first message; when someone calls the number, the AI picks up and follows the prompt. Pass `notify.emailTo` to also receive a plain-English summary of every call (caller, intent, urgency, callback time) so small-business owners do not need to read transcripts. Free tier: 5 minutes/month free trial, no card required. Once the trial is exhausted, inbound AI calls hang up until the 1st of next month (UTC) or until the user upgrades to Pro for unlimited inbound AI at $0.40/min. Available on US and Canada numbers. Before writing systemPrompt from scratch, fetch GET https://api.agentcall.co/v1/calls/prompt-templates (no auth) for pre-built templates: receptionist, lead qualifier, appointment booker, customer support, call screener. Preview voices via GET https://api.agentcall.co/v1/calls/voices.

IMPORTANT (persistent config: confirm before enabling, monitor after): this is NOT a one-shot tool call. Once configured, the number autonomously answers every incoming call from then on, accumulates per-minute charges (at $0.40/min on Pro past the Free 5-minute monthly trial), and keeps doing so until disable_inbound_ai is called. Before invoking, get the user's explicit confirmation on: (1) exact E.164 number to configure (read it back), (2) systemPrompt content (read back at least the WHAT WE DO and PRICING sections so the user can correct invented facts before any caller hears them), (3) record setting (default false; recording adds $0.01/min and 1-year retention), (4) notify.emailTo (confirm the address is theirs and that they want every call summarized), (5) maxDurationSecs (use the smallest value that fits the use case so a stuck call cannot run for an hour), (6) budget expectations (Free 5 min/month then hang-up, or Pro $0.40/min with no monthly cap). After enabling, periodically check get_usage so the user sees consumption, and proactively offer to call disable_inbound_ai when their stated goal is met (testing finished, campaign over, business closed for the season). On reports of unexpected charges or unexpected calls, disable_inbound_ai first then debug.

numberId.description: The phone number ID to configure for inbound AI (e.g. 'num_abc123')

systemPrompt.description: Instructions for the AI when it answers (e.g. 'You are the front desk for Acme Plumbing. Greet callers, take their name, phone, and a brief description of the issue, then say someone will call back within 2 hours.')

voice.description: Voice: 10 options. marin (soft, natural) and cedar (warm, grounded) are the newest natural-sounding picks. shimmer (default), sage, ash, ballad, coral, echo, verse, alloy. Preview at GET /v1/calls/voices.

firstMessage.description: What the AI says when it picks up (e.g. 'Hi, thanks for calling Acme Plumbing. How can I help?')

maxDurationSecs.description: Max call duration in seconds (default 600, max 3600)

record.description: Opt in to call recording. When true, every inbound call to this number is recorded and listenable from the dashboard. Adds $0.01/min on top of the AI voice rate. Tell the user to make sure their first message includes a TCPA disclosure ('this call may be recorded for quality') — required by two-party-consent states. Default false.

emailTo.description: Email address to receive the post-call summary

businessName.description: Business name shown in the email subject and body

agencyName.description: Agency/brand name used in the email sign-off

notify.description: Optional post-call email notification. After every call, AgentCall summarizes the transcript with an LLM and emails the summary to the configured address. Spam calls are auto-suppressed. Email-only for v1; SMS notifications are not exposed.

function.name: mcp_agentcall_create_webhook
function.description: Subscribe to outbound event delivery: AgentCall POSTs to your HTTPS URL when events fire (SMS received, OTP detected, call completed, etc.). NOT for handling inbound calls — to make a phone number answer incoming calls, use 'configure_inbound_ai' instead. The URL must use HTTPS.

function.name: mcp_agentcall_delete_webhook
function.description: Deactivate and remove a webhook endpoint. It will immediately stop receiving events.

function.name: mcp_agentcall_disable_inbound_ai
function.description: Disable inbound AI on a phone number. Future incoming calls will be hung up by the carrier until re-configured. Does not release the number — billing for the number itself continues.

numberId.description: The phone number ID to clear inbound config for

function.name: mcp_agentcall_get_call
function.description: Get details of a specific call by ID, including status, duration, and recording URL.

callId.description: The call ID to look up (e.g. 'call_abc123')

function.name: mcp_agentcall_get_call_transcript
function.description: Get the transcript of a completed AI voice call. Returns the full conversation with timestamps, a summary, and call duration.

callId.description: The AI call ID to get the transcript for

function.name: mcp_agentcall_get_inbox
function.description: Get inbound SMS messages for a phone number. Can filter to show only messages containing OTP codes.

numberId.description: The phone number ID to check inbox for

otpOnly.description: If true, only return messages that contain an extracted OTP code

function.name: mcp_agentcall_get_message
function.description: Get a specific SMS message by its ID. Returns the full message including body, OTP (if extracted), and status.

function.name: mcp_agentcall_get_number
function.description: Get details of a specific phone number by its ID. Returns the full number object including status and monthly rate.

function.name: mcp_agentcall_get_prompt
function.description: Get a prompt by name from MCP server 'agentcall'

name.description: Name of the prompt to retrieve

arguments.description: Optional arguments to pass to the prompt

function.name: mcp_agentcall_get_usage
function.description: Get usage and cost breakdown for a billing period. Returns numbers provisioned, SMS sent/received, call minutes, recording minutes, and total cost.

period.description: Billing period in YYYY-MM format (e.g. '2026-02'). Defaults to current month.

function.name: mcp_agentcall_hangup_call
function.description: Terminate an active phone call. The call must be in progress.

callId.description: The call ID to hang up (e.g. 'call_abc123')

function.name: mcp_agentcall_initiate_ai_call
function.description: Start an AI-powered voice call where AI handles the conversation autonomously. The AI will speak using the specified voice and follow the system prompt instructions. Pro plan only ($0.40/min). Available on US and Canada numbers. Before writing systemPrompt from scratch, fetch GET https://api.agentcall.co/v1/calls/prompt-templates (no auth) for ready-made templates with placeholders to fill in.

from.description: The number ID or E.164 phone string to call from (must be a US or Canada number)

to.description: Recipient phone number in E.164 format (e.g. '[REDACTED_PHONE]')

systemPrompt.description: Instructions for the AI during the call (e.g. 'You are calling a restaurant to ask about their hours and reservation policy')

voice.description: Voice: 10 options. marin (soft, natural) and cedar (warm, grounded) are the newest natural-sounding picks. shimmer (default), sage, ash, ballad, coral, echo, verse, alloy. Preview at GET /v1/calls/voices.

firstMessage.description: What the AI says first when the call connects (e.g. 'Hello! I'm calling to ask about your business hours.')

maxDurationSecs.description: Max call duration in seconds (default 600, max 3600)

record.description: Opt in to call recording. When true, both legs of the call are recorded and listenable from the dashboard. Adds $0.01/min on top of the AI voice rate. Make sure firstMessage includes a TCPA disclosure ('this call may be recorded for quality') — required by two-party-consent states. Default false.

function.name: mcp_agentcall_initiate_call
function.description: Start an outbound phone call from one of your provisioned numbers. Optionally enable call recording (Pro plan only, $0.01/min).

from.description: The number ID or E.164 phone string to call from (must be a provisioned number)

to.description: Recipient phone number in E.164 format (e.g. '[REDACTED_PHONE]')

record.description: Enable call recording (Pro plan only, $0.01/min additional)

function.name: mcp_agentcall_list_calls
function.description: List call history for this agent. Returns calls with their status, duration, and recording URLs.

function.name: mcp_agentcall_list_numbers
function.description: List all phone numbers provisioned for this agent. Returns an array of number objects with IDs, phone strings, types, and status.

function.name: mcp_agentcall_list_prompts
function.description: List available prompts from MCP server 'agentcall'

function.name: mcp_agentcall_list_webhooks
function.description: List all registered webhook endpoints and their subscribed events.

function.name: mcp_agentcall_provision_number
function.description: Provision a new phone number for your AI agent. Returns the number details including the phone number string and ID.

country.description: Country code — US or CA. Defaults to US.

type.description: Number type. 'local' ($2/mo, available on Free + Pro). 'mobile' ($2/mo) and 'tollfree' ($2.50/mo, 800-style numbers) are Pro plan only.

areaCode.description: Optional 3-digit NANP area code for local or mobile numbers. Works for both US (e.g. '314' St. Louis, '415' SF) and CA (e.g. '416' Toronto, '604' Vancouver). Canada shares NANP. Only honored for local and mobile types. Ignored for tollfree. Returns a no_numbers_in_area_code error if inventory is empty for that NPA. Fall back to omitting for any local number in the country.

function.name: mcp_agentcall_release_number
function.description: Release a phone number, deactivating it and stopping billing. This action is irreversible.

numberId.description: The phone number ID to release

function.name: mcp_agentcall_rotate_webhook_secret
function.description: Rotate the signing secret for a webhook. The old secret is immediately invalidated and a new one is generated. Store the new secret securely — it is only shown once.

webhookId.description: The webhook ID to rotate the secret for (e.g. 'wh_abc123')

function.name: mcp_agentcall_send_sms
function.description: Send an SMS message from one of your provisioned numbers. Returns the message object with ID and delivery status.

from.description: The number ID or E.164 phone string to send from (must be a provisioned number)

to.description: Recipient phone number in E.164 format (e.g. '[REDACTED_PHONE]')

function.name: mcp_agentcall_update_number_label
function.description: Rename a phone number by updating its label. The label is a human-readable name shown in the dashboard and number listings (e.g. 'Hermes assistant', 'Ace Plumbing line'). Pass null to clear the label. Does not change the phone number itself — only the label.

numberId.description: The phone number ID to rename

label.description: New label (max 100 chars), or null to clear the label

function.name: mcp_agentcall_wait_for_otp
function.description: Wait for an OTP verification code to arrive via SMS on a phone number. Polls the inbox until an OTP is detected or timeout is reached. Returns the extracted code string.

numberId.description: The phone number ID to watch for incoming OTP

timeout.description: Max wait time in milliseconds (default 60000 = 60 seconds)

function.name: patch
function.description:
Targeted find-and-replace edits in files. Use this instead of sed/awk in terminal. Uses fuzzy matching (9 strategies) so minor whitespace/indentation differences won't break it. Returns a unified diff. Auto-runs syntax checks after editing.

REPLACE MODE (mode='replace', default): find a unique string and replace it. REQUIRED PARAMETERS: mode, path, old_string, new_string.
PATCH MODE (mode='patch'): apply V4A multi-file patches for bulk changes. REQUIRED PARAMETERS: mode, patch.

mode.description: Edit mode. 'replace' (default): requires path + old_string + new_string. 'patch': requires patch content only.

path.description: REQUIRED when mode='replace'. File path to edit.

old_string.description: REQUIRED when mode='replace'. Exact text to find and replace. Must be unique in the file unless replace_all=true. Include surrounding context lines to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: REQUIRED when mode='replace'. Replacement text. Pass empty string '' to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: Replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false)

patch.description:
REQUIRED when mode='patch'. V4A format patch content. Format:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: path/to/file
@@ context hint @@
 context line
-removed line
+added line
*** End Patch

function.name: read_file
function.description: Read a text file with line numbers and pagination. Use this instead of cat/head/tail in terminal. Output format: 'LINE_NUM|CONTENT'. Suggests similar filenames if not found. Use offset and limit for large files. Reads exceeding ~100K characters are rejected; use offset and limit to read specific sections of large files. NOTE: Cannot read images or binary files — use vision_analyze for images.

path.description: Path to the file to read (absolute, relative, or ~/path)

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed, default: 1)

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read (default: 500, max: 2000)

function.name: search_files
function.description:
Search file contents or find files by name. Use this instead of grep/rg/find/ls in terminal. Ripgrep-backed, faster than shell equivalents.

Content search (target='content'): Regex search inside files. Output modes: full matches with line numbers, file paths only, or match counts.

File search (target='files'): Find files by glob pattern (e.g., '*.py', '*config*'). Also use this instead of ls — results sorted by modification time.

pattern.description: Regex pattern for content search, or glob pattern (e.g., '*.py') for file search

target.description: 'content' searches inside file contents, 'files' searches for files by name

path.description: Directory or file to search in (default: current working directory)

file_glob.description: Filter files by pattern in grep mode (e.g., '*.py' to only search Python files)

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 50)

offset.description: Skip first N results for pagination (default: 0)

output_mode.description: Output format for grep mode: 'content' shows matching lines with line numbers, 'files_only' lists file paths, 'count' shows match counts per file

context.description: Number of context lines before and after each match (grep mode only)

function.name: write_file
function.description: Write content to a file, completely replacing existing content. Use this instead of echo/cat heredoc in terminal. Creates parent directories automatically. OVERWRITES the entire file — use 'patch' for targeted edits. Auto-runs syntax checks on .py/.json/.yaml/.toml and other linted languages; only NEW errors introduced by this write are surfaced (pre-existing errors are filtered out).

path.description: Path to the file to write (will be created if it doesn't exist, overwritten if it does)

content.description: Complete content to write to the file

messages.content: Refactor 3 admin pages in /root/elviajero to use shared UI components and fix issues. Read each file first, understand it, then rewrite it.
